audit_status.source_base: Greek Galatians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for freedom, circumcision warning, trust working through love, Spirit versus flesh-pattern, love of neighbor, works of flesh and fruit of Spirit.

verses.restored: For freedom Messiah set us free; stand firm therefore and do not be held again in a yoke of slavery.
Look, I Paul say to you that if you receive circumcision, Messiah will benefit you nothing.
And I testify again to every human receiving circumcision that one is obligated to do the whole Law.
You were made inactive from Messiah, whoever are being made righteous/just by Law; you fell from grace.
For we, by Spirit, from trust, eagerly await the hope of righteousness/justice.
For in Messiah Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision has any strength, but trust working through love.
You were running beautifully/well; who hindered you from being persuaded by the truth?
This persuasion is not from the One calling you.
A little leaven leavens the whole lump.
I am persuaded about you in the Lord that you will think nothing otherwise; but the one troubling you will bear the judgment, whoever that may be.
But I, siblings, if I still proclaim circumcision, why am I still pursued/persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been made inactive.
Would that those unsettling you would even cut themselves off.
For you were called to freedom, siblings; only not freedom as a starting point for flesh, but through love serve one another.
For the whole Law has been fulfilled in one word, in this: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
But if you bite and devour one another, watch lest you be consumed by one another.
But I say: walk by Spirit, and you will certainly not complete the desire of flesh.
For the flesh desires against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; for these are opposed to one another, so that you may not do whatever you want.
But if you are led by Spirit, you are not under Law.
Now manifest are the works of the flesh: sexual immorality/misuse, uncleanness, shameless excess,
idolatry, sorcery, enmities, strife, jealousy, angers, selfish ambitions, dissensions, factions,
envies, drunkennesses, revelries, and things like these, which I tell you beforehand, as I said before, that those practicing such things will not inherit the reign of the Divine.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness/trust,
gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no Law.
But those of Messiah Jesus crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
If we live by Spirit, let us also walk in line by Spirit.
Let us not become empty-glorious, provoking one another, envying one another.

translation_notes: Galatians 5:6 is a key anchor: neither circumcision nor uncircumcision, but trust working through love.
Freedom is not license for flesh-pattern; it becomes loving service.
Works of flesh are patterns of destructive desire and community fracture, not a condemnation of embodiment itself.
